- Sie sind bestürt und haben eine Nischefamilie.
- Für zwei Personen sind 125 Quadratmeter ein bisschen viel.
- Wir haben ein Stuhl, kommen keiner, denken keiner auf Besuch,
- und dann ist das Haus voll.
- Look at photos, read all the text anyway.

- Zwei.
- Ech schaffe an der Stadt an ech wunne mat menger Frëndin an engem Appartement zu Miersch.
- Miersch ass ongeféier zwanzeg Kilometer vun der Stadt Lütze-Busche wesch.
- Dat ass net wäit an ech kann den Zug huelen fir op der Best.
- Dat ass ganz praktisch.
- Meng Frëndin ass wäide Puppelchen a schafft de Moment net.
- Fir zwei Personen an ekand fannemiet Appartement vu fünfundsich Quadratmeter e büsse kleng.
- Two.
- I work in town and I live with my girlfriend in an apartment in Miersch.
- Miersch is about twenty kilometers from the city of Lütze-Busche.
- That’s not far and I can take the train to the Best.
- It’s very convenient.
- My girlfriend is a busy little girl and isn’t working at the moment.
- For two people, a small apartment of about fifty-three square meters is a bit small.

- Wéi vill Leit wunnen an der WG?
- Dräi.
- Wat studéiert Dir?
- Ech studéieren Ekonomie op der Uni Lëtzebuerg um Lampertsbierg.
- Wou wunnen Dir?
- Ech wunnen mat dräi anere Studenten an enger WG zu Mamer.
- Wéi grouss ass den Appartement?
- Mir deelen en Appartement vun 100 Metercarré.
- Wéi grouss sinn d'Zëmmer?
- All Persoun huet en Zëmmer tëscht 14 an 20 Metercarré.
- Fonctionéiert d'Kollokatioun gutt?
- Jo, d'Kollokatioun fonctionéiert ganz gutt.
- Ass et lueg oder deier zu Feier?
- Zu Feier ass et lueg an et zu deier.
- How many people live in the shared flat?
- Three.
- What are you studying?
- I am studying Economics at the University of Luxembourg at the Lampertsbierg.
- Where do you live?
- I live with three other students in a shared flat in Mamer.
- How big is the apartment?
- We share an apartment of 100 square meters.
- How big are the rooms?
- Each person has a room between 14 and 20 square meters.
- Is the shared living arrangement working well?
- Yes, the shared living arrangement works very well.
- Is it cheap or expensive in Luxembourg?
- In Luxembourg it is cheap and also expensive.

- Feier.
- Es sig geschéit, an es sich nëmmen eng nei Wunneng.
- Dat ass net einfach.
- De Moment wunnen ech bei mengen Elteren.
- Si hunn en Haus vun ongeféier 160 Quadratmeter zu Wolz.
- Si hu keng direkt Noperen a vill Terrain.
- Dat ass flott, mee et ass komesch, erëm bei den Elteren ze wunnen.
- Celebration.
- It is said, and it is only a new apartment.
- That is not easy.
- At the moment I live with my parents.
- They have a house of about 160 square meters in Wolz.
- They have no direct neighbors and a lot of land.
- That is nice, but it is strange to live with the parents again.

- Ech wunnen an engem Studio zu Bouneweg.
- An der Résidence wunnen nach vill aner Leit, mä ech kenne just meng direkt Noperen.
- Si si ganz fein, an heiansdo invitéieren si mech owes z'etessen.
- Mäi Studio vun drësseg Meter Carré ass ze kleng fir vill Leit z'invitéieren,
- mä grouss genuch fir eng Persoun.
- I live in a studio apartment in Bouneweg.
- And at the residence live many other people, but I only know my direct neighbors.
- They are very nice, and occasionally they invite me over to eat.
- My thirty-square-meter studio is too small to invite many people,
- but big enough for one person.

- Liest aleu staten Dialog.
- Kuckemol, weifennst du da d'appartement hei?
- Et ass zu Äschternach.
- Et ass groß an net zu daier.
- Äschternach, dat ass ze weit.
- An net wird kei Balkon.
- An dat hei?
- Et ass zu Stroossen, et ass jo no bei der Stadt.
- Et wird ein groß Kischen an e Balkon.
- Ja, Kischen ass groß, mit d'appartement wird just 75 Quadratmeter.
- Dat ass zu kleng.
- An net ass daier, an net wird mol keng Parkplätz.
- An net ass keng Foto bei der Annonce.
- Hmm, eng Wunningssichen ass net einfach.
- Ech fannen da d'appartement heiflut.
- Et ass net belesch, mee et wird eng oppe Kischen, dreizimmeren, e Buurzimmer an eng separat Toilette.
- Et wird durch e Balkon an enger Garage.
- Wo ass et?
- Zu Sandweiler.
- Ah, interessant, mee et ass im dritte Stack an et wird kei Lift.
- Dat fannes net praktisch.
- Oh, du hasch recht, dat ass wirklich net praktisch.
- Read all state dialogues.
- Check once, where is the apartment here?
- It is in Eschternach.
- It is big and not too dear.
- Eschernach, that is too far.
- And there will not be a balcony.
- And that here?
- It is on a street, it is still near the city.
- It will be a large kitchen and a balcony.
- Yes, the kitchen is large, with the apartment just 75 square meters.
- That is too small.
- And not is dear, and not will there be any parking.
- And there is no photo in the advertisement.
- Hmm, a housing search is not easy.
- I find the apartment here helpful.
- It is not popular, but there will be a small kitchen, three rooms, a living room and a separate toilet.
- It will have a balcony and a garage.
- Where is it?
- In Sandweiler.
- Ah, interesting, but it is on the third floor and there will be no elevator.
- That is not practical.
- Oh, you are right, that is really not practical.

- Bonjour, mäi Numm ass Nathalie Steichen.
- Ech hunn Är Annonce an der Zeitung gesinn.
- Dir hutt eng Wunneng bei Dikrech ze verlounen. Ass dat richteg?
- Jo.
- Ass d'Appartement nach fräi?
- Jo, mee vläicht sinn interesséiert.
- Vu wou ass et genee?
- Et ass zu Eppelduerf, net wäit vun Ettelbreck an Dikrech.
- Weigroß ass et?
- 54 Quadratmeter.
- Wéi vill ass de Loyé?
- 567 Euro mat de Chargen.
- Gëtt et eng Garage?
- Nee, mee fir 30 Euro de Mount kënnt dir eng Parkplaz lounen.
- De private Parking ass direkt bei der Entrée vum Appartementshaus.
- Sinn Hausdieren erlaabt?
- Ech hunn en Katz.
- Jo, dat ass kee Problem.
- Ech si ganz interesséiert, kënne mir Rendezvous maache fir eng Visite?
- Jo, natierlech.
- Hello, my name is Nathalie Steichen.
- I saw your ad in the newspaper.
- You have an apartment for rent in Dikrech, right?
- Yes.
- Is the apartment still free?
- Yes, but I might be interested.
- Where is it exactly from?
- It is in Eppelduerf, not far from Ettelbruck and Dikrech.
- How big is it?
- 54 square meters.
- How much is the rent?
- 567 Euro with charges.
- Is there a garage?
- No, but for 30 Euro per month you can rent a parking space.
- The private parking is right at the entrance of the apartment building.
- Are pets allowed?
- I have a cat.
- Yes, that’s not a problem.
- I am very interested, can we arrange a meeting for a viewing?
- Yes, of course.

- Hei hunn ech en Haus zu Fayenen.
- Et ass schéin a modern, an et huet keng direkt Noperen.
- Um Rez-de-Chaussée gëtt et eng Kichen, en Iesszëmmer, eng Stuff an eng separat Toilette.
- Um éischte Stack sinn Buedzëmmer an dräi Zëmmeren.
- D'Haus huet kee Balcon, mee e Gaart an eng flott Terrasse.
- Et huet kee Keller, mee eng grouss Garage an e Späicher.
- Interesséiert dech dat?
- I have a house in Fayenne.
- It is nice and modern, and it has no direct neighbors.
- On the ground floor there is a kitchen, a dining room, a living room, and a separate toilet.
- On the first floor there are bathrooms and three bedrooms.
- The house has no balcony, but a garden and a nice terrace.
- It has no cellar, but a large garage and a storage room.
- Are you interested in that?

- Wat wöht aus?
- Lauschtert a kreizt un.
- Mei Haus ass kleng, mee ganz gemittlech.
- Um Rez-de-Chaussee sinn Kichen, Stuff an eng separat Toilette.
- Et gëtt keen Iesszëmmer.
- Meesst normalerweis an der Kichen.
- Wann mer Besuch hun, iessme an der Stuff.
- Um éischte Stack sinn Bootszimmer an zwee Schlofzëmmeren,
- lenks ass d'Elterschlofzëmmer,
- a rechts ass d'Kannerschlofzëmmer.
- Mir hun e klengen Gaart, mee mir hun keng Terrasse.
- An der Garage ass Plaz fir den Auto a fir Sachen ze stockéieren.
- Mir hun kee Keller a kee Speicher.
- What does it matter?
- Listen and cross-check.
- My house is small, but very cozy.
- On the ground floor there are kitchen, living room and a separate toilet.
- There is no dining room.
- Mostly in the kitchen.
- When we have guests, we eat in the living room.
- On the first floor are a laundry room and two bedrooms,
- on the left is the parents' bedroom,
- and on the right is the children's bedroom.
- We have a small garden, but we have no terrace.
- In the garage there is space for the car and for storing things.
- We have no basement and no attic.

- Marc, hei ass de Butzplang vun eiser Wunngemeinschaft.
- E Butzplang?
- A wat ass dat dann?
- Maja, mir wunne jo elo zu Feierheiz zesummen, a mir delen eis d'Stuff, Bötzemer a Kichen.
- Hei um Plang si Feier grouss Butzaktivitéiten, an du bass all Woch fir eng aner Saach zoustänneg.
- Aha. Wat sinn dann déi Feier Butzaktivitéiten?
- Eestens Kichebutzen, zweetens Bötzemerbutzen, drëttens Stufstëbsen a Staubsaugen, a feiertens Dreckskëschten an Recyclinkëschten eidelmachen.
- Kichebutzen? Wat heescht dat?
- Eng ganz Woch lang spulen an alles proper machen.
- Ech spule guer net gär.
- Nee, nee. Jiddereen spult seng Saach riss awer.
- Kichebutzen heescht de Bodem staubsaugen an Nassa butzen an enkéiert Kochblock an Mikrowell proper maachen.
- Dat maachen ech och alles net gär.
- Ech wölle mich leiver all Woch ëm d'Dreckskischt an de Recyclinkëschten këmmeren.
- Geet dat?
- Nee, dat geet net. Mir hunn Hullemont. Mir maachen all ne demensgerde Stoot. Mätwohneng soll jo awer proper bleiwen.
- Okay, ech verstinn. Wou ass d'Butzgeschir?
- Dat ass am Kaller am Schaf bei der Waschmaschinn.
- Do steet doch de Staubsauger, e Strackbriet an e Strackäusen.
- O mei, Stracke fannen ech schrecklech.
- Ech stracke ni.
- Marc, this is the butt plan for our shared living community.
- A butt plan?
- And what is that then?
- Maja, we now live together in Feierheiz, and we share our stuff, Bötzemer and kitchen.
- On the plan, the Feier activities are big butt activities, and you are every week responsible for another thing.
- Aha. What are then the Feier butt activities?
- First kitchen cleaning, second Bötzemer cleaning, third vacuuming and dusting, and fifth emptying trash and recycling bins.
- Kitchen cleaning? What does that mean?
- A whole week of cleaning and making everything proper.
- I don’t like cleaning at all.
- No, no. Everyone cleans their own thing, though.
- Kitchen cleaning means vacuuming the floor and wiping the sink and making the cooking block and microwave clean.
- I don’t like doing all that either.
- I’d rather take care of the trash and recycling bins every week.
- Is that possible?
- No, that doesn’t work. We have a schedule. We do every other week. The apartment should stay tidy, after all.
- Okay, I understand. Where is the butt laundry?
- It’s in the kitchen, near the washing machine.
- There you find the vacuum, a mop, and a broom.
- Oh my, I hate mops.
- I never mop.

- Art.

- Mojen, ech hunn e Luchtflott.
- Wievill kascht se?
- 20 Euro.
- Se ass net al, also funktionéiert.
- Ech hu lese fir 15 Euro.
- Averständ.
- A wat kascht den Dësch?
- Waat fir een?
- Den éischten oder den zweeten Dësch?
- Den éischten.
- 100 Euro.
- Dat ass ze deier.
- En ass och nach net al.
- Ech hunn en eréischt zwee Joer an en huet 1200 Euro kascht.
- En ass jo schéin a gutt an der Rei.
- Eréischt zwee Joer?
- Fir wat verkeeft dir en dann?
- All meng Saache si relativ nei.
- Ech plënner bei meng Frëndin an hat huet scho en alles.
- Seng Saache si méi modern wéi meng.
- A sou?
- Hud dir nach aner interessant Saachen?
- Jo, sécher.
- Haya seng Kaffis-Maschine fir 50 Euro an eng Tölle fir 150 Euro.
- Dat ass e super Geschäft.
- Saache si normalerweis vill méi deier an en ass eng ganz gutt Qualitéit.
- Hmm.
- Hei hunn ech och nach eng Fotell.
- Fotell ass e bëssi méi al, mee ganz gemittlech.
- Se ass net deier.
- Ech verkeefe se fir 25 Euro.
- Ech wees net.
- Ech kucke nach e bëssen.
- Nach e schéinen Dag.
- Mersi gläichfalls.
- Adi.
- Hello, I have a floating lamp.
- How much does it cost?
- 20 euro.
- It’s not old either, so it works.
- I have read for 15 euro.
- Understood.
- And what does the table cost?
- What for a one?
- The first or the second table?
- The first.
- 100 euro.
- That’s too expensive.
- One is not old either.
- I have one already two years and it cost 1200 euro.
- It’s nice and in good shape.
- Two years already?
- What are you selling it for then?
- All my things are relatively new.
- I’m moving in with my girlfriend and already have everything.
- Her things are more modern than mine.
- And so?
- Do you have any other interesting items?
- Yes, of course.
- Haya’s coffee machine for 50 euro and a kettle for 150 euro.
- That’s a great deal.
- Things are usually much more expensive and one is of very good quality.
- Hmm.
- Here I also have another chair.
- The chair is a bit older, but very comfy.
- It’s not expensive.
- I’m selling it for 25 euro.
- I don’t know.
- I’ll look a bit more.
- Have a nice day.
- Thanks you too.
- Bye.
